title: ExtruderMachineElement Object
description: Machine element representing an extruder on a fused filament fabrication (FFF) machine. A machine can have multiple extruders and thus multiple ExtruderMachineElement elements.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Delete this extruder element from the machine. Throws an exception when trying to delete the last remaining element.

methods.name: staticTypeId
methods.description: Identifying name for all elements of this type. Pass this to the itemByType or itemById methods of MachineElements to filter to elements of this type.

properties.name: elementId
properties.description: Identifier for this element. Unique within an element type.

properties.name: filamentDiameter
properties.description: Filament diameter of this extruder in cm.

properties.name: isFanAvailable
properties.description: Flag indicating if a fan, whose speed is settable in the post, is available.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of this extruder. Depending on the post, this may be output in the resulting gcode as a comment.

properties.name: nozzleDiameter
properties.description: Nozzle diameter of this extruder in cm.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Offset relative to the main extruder. The first extruder has an index of 0 and usually an offset of (0,0,0).

properties.name: temperature
properties.description: The maximum temperature this extruder can reach in degrees C.

properties.name: typeId
properties.description: Identifier for this type of machine element.

properties.name: volumePerSecond
properties.description: The maximum volume output measured in cm^3/s.

classType.title: ExtruderMachineElement.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: ExtruderMachineElement.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Delete this extruder element from the machine. Throws an exception when trying to delete the last remaining element.

staticTypeId.title: ExtruderMachineElement.staticTypeId Method
staticTypeId.description: Identifying name for all elements of this type. Pass this to the itemByType or itemById methods of MachineElements to filter to elements of this type.

elementId.title: ExtruderMachineElement.elementId Property
elementId.description: Identifier for this element. Unique within an element type.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = extruderMachineElement_var.elementId

filamentDiameter.description: Filament diameter of this extruder in cm.

# Set the value of the property.
